key_departures.impact: Led the team with 1,013 receiving yards in 2025 — the first Patriot to crack 1,000 yards since Julian Edelman in 2019. Released due to cap hit escalating from $10.5M to $26.5M in 2026. Carries $9.7M dead cap. Leaves a massive void as WR1 for Drake Maye.

Career-best season as New England's top nose tackle — 17 quarterback pressures, strong run-stopper at 52% pass rush snap rate. Was a versatile piece also used as short-yardage fullback. Departed on a 3-year, $21M deal with $14M guaranteed. Cory Durden is next man up but is a significant downgrade.

Served as quality backup LT, filling in admirably when Will Campbell suffered a knee injury. Signed a 2-year, $12M max deal ($5.75M guaranteed) with SF. Leaves New England without a proven backup behind the developing Will Campbell, with 35-year-old Morgan Moses at RT.
Departed on a 1-year, $3.25M deal back to Atlanta. Was a depth piece and blocking TE. Leaves Hunter Henry as the clear TE1 with limited proven depth behind him. Creates a need for a TE2/backup.

Breakout season: career-high 7.5 sacks, 54 pressures, 64.9% snap share. Led Patriots in pressures (74 including playoffs). Was their most productive pass rusher and is now a UFA. His departure (if confirmed) is the most damaging loss on defense — leaves a massive hole at starting EDGE opposite Harold Landry. NFL execs project market value of $8–11M/year.

Elevated from backup to full-time starter in 2025, finishing with 4 INTs (tied for team lead), 91.3% defensive snap share, 85 tackles. A UFA who Patriots are still in active talks with as of March 10. If he departs, leaves a massive void next to rookie Craig Woodson. Status: UNRESOLVED — negotiations ongoing.

Released in February after tearing his ACL in Week 5 of 2025. Was primarily a kick returner and rotational RB. Leaves a hole in the return game specifically; RB depth (Rhamondre Stevenson, TreVeyon Henderson) is adequate.

Traded for 2027 5th-round pick. Started all 21 games at center in 2025. Jared Wilson (3rd-round pick in 2025) slides from LG to center to replace him. Creates a vacancy at LG — addressed by Vera-Tucker signing. Net IOL impact is managed but watch depth.
Released to create cap space ($1.33M in 2026, $5.32M in 2027). Was a core special teamer and rotational linebacker. Depth loss only — Jack Gibbens and the existing LB corps absorbs this.
Patriots declined to tender as RFA — he becomes a UFA. Was CB5 on the depth chart. Depth loss only; Christian Gonzalez, Carlton Davis III, Marcus Jones are all under contract.

signings.analysis: Career-high 7 sacks and 55 pressures in 2025, split between Tennessee and Baltimore. Gets $14.5M in Year 1, all $23.1M guaranteed is front-loaded in first two years — giving the Patriots two real years of commitment. Jones is a versatile 6'3", 281-lb hybrid who can play outside or inside. At $13.2M AAV he's mid-tier for EDGE, and he addresses the immediate starter need, but he's not a pure wide-9 speed rusher. With Chaisson still unsigned, EDGE remains a need — Jones fills one spot opposite Landry, but the group still lacks depth and an elite closer. This is a partial fix, not a solution. Need drops from critical to moderate.

3-year deal worth $42M base with a maximum value of $48M (guarantees not yet fully reported — estimated significant per structure). Slots immediately as LG, freeing Jared Wilson to slide to center (replacing traded Bradbury). When healthy, AVT is a top-10 guard — PFF graded him 10th-best guard with the 4th-lowest pressure rate (2.8%) in 2024. Major health risk: he has missed 41 of 85 career games including all of 2025 with a torn triceps. The 3-year investment signals real commitment. Assuming health, IOL need is resolved — Wilson at C, AVT at LG, and the existing RG fill the interior. Need fully addressed on paper with significant injury caveat.
signings.notes: Contract guarantees not fully reported as of 2026-03-10. Maximum value $48M per Adam Schefter/ESPN.

signings.analysis: Fullback signing — not a traditional RB need position. Gilliam was arguably the NFL's best FB in 2025, opening holes for Bills RB James Cook (NFL's leading rusher). 3-year deal with $6M fully guaranteed is a real investment in a specific role. He fills a gap in Josh McDaniels' offense where a lead blocker was identified as a missing ingredient in 2025. Signing noted as a schematic fit, not addressing a draft-need position. $4M AAV at FB is well above market — this is their FB1. Also handles special teams (77% ST snap rate in 2025). No direct need adjustment — it's a position-specific luxury fill.

signings.previous_team: Arizona Cardinals (spent 2025 on LA Rams practice squad)
signings.analysis: 1-year minimum deal. Adds to a crowded EDGE room (Landry, Jones, Jennings, Ponder, Swinson) but does not address the starter need. Depth/camp body signing. EDGE need is unchanged at the starter level.

Re-signed as QB3 behind Drake Maye (starter) and Joshua Dobbs (QB2, under contract). Did not take a snap in 2025 but was a praised practice asset. 2-year deal retains scout-team value and keeps the QB room intact. QB is not a need — Drake Maye is locked in long-term. No need adjustment.

ERFA tender. Westover is a hybrid FB/TE who contributed when starter Brock Lampe went down in 2025. With Hooper gone, he's now TE2/3. Minimum tender — does not solve the TE depth need but retains a known commodity. TE need remains largely unchanged.

recommended_need_adjustments.reasoning: Stefon Diggs released (1,013 yards in 2025, now carrying $9.7M dead cap). No WR signed. Patriots failed to land Alec Pierce (4yr/$116M, Colts), missed on Rashid Shaheed (Seahawks re-sign), not in on Mike Evans. Romeo Doubs interest is described as 'unlikely' by multiple sources. Drake Maye has no established WR1 for 2026. This is now the most urgent need on the roster and the most glaring remaining hole entering the draft.

Dre'Mont Jones signing (3yr/$39.5M, $23.1M gtd) partially addresses the EDGE need — he's a legitimate starter with 7 career sacks and versatility. But K'Lavon Chaisson (10.5 sacks, 74 pressures in 2025) remains unsigned and unreplaced. Harold Landry is the other starter (coming off knee injury, under contract through 2027). Jones + Landry is a serviceable but not elite pairing — still lacks a true closer/speed rusher. Team ranked 26th in sacks (35) and 26th in pressures (121) in 2025. Drops from Tier 1 to Tier 2 — still a real need especially if Chaisson signs elsewhere.

Alijah Vera-Tucker (3yr/$42M) slots as LG. Jared Wilson (2025 3rd-rounder) moves from LG to C replacing Bradbury. Guard need was the IOL priority and AVT directly fills it. Wilson's transition to C is a short-term question mark but he's a proven 2025 starter. Need drops to depth-only — a veteran backup IOL for insurance would be prudent but this is no longer a draft priority.

Jaylinn Hawkins (4 INTs, 91.3% snap share in 2025) remains unsigned as of March 10. Patriots are in active talks but no deal confirmed. Craig Woodson (2025 4th-rounder) is the other starting safety under contract. If Hawkins departs, S escalates to Tier 1 — a starting-caliber veteran safety must be found in FA or the draft. If Hawkins re-signs, S stabilizes to Tier 3/4. Holding at Tier 2 pending resolution — revisit when/if Hawkins situation is decided.

Khyiris Tonga (3yr/$21M, Chiefs) was the Patriots' top nose tackle and a critical early-down run defender. Cory Durden is the next man up but is a lesser player. Milton Williams and Christian Barmore (charges dropped) are interior options but New England lacks a true run-stuffing nose. Need escalates from depth concern to a genuine starter vacancy. Interior DL should be a Day 2 draft target.

Vederian Lowe signed with SF (2yr/$12M) — he was the primary backup LT behind Will Campbell. Campbell (health concerns, developing LT) is the starter; 35-year-old Morgan Moses is at RT and aging. The team now has thin depth behind two tackle spots with legitimate durability/age concerns. Marcus Bryant is a developmental option but an unproven one. A veteran OT via FA or a Day 2 draft pick is needed.

Austin Hooper departed (Falcons, 1yr/$3.25M). Jack Westover retained via ERFA tender but is a minimum-level depth piece. Hunter Henry is the TE1 but entering the final year of his contract (no extension yet). Potential Patriots TE targets (Cade Otton re-signed with TB; Charlie Kolar went to Chargers; Isaiah Likely went to NYG) all went elsewhere. TE2/TE3 depth is now very thin. A TE in the middle rounds of the draft or a remaining FA target is needed.

Christian Gonzalez (extension imminent, per reporting) and Carlton Davis III anchor the CB room. Marcus Jones is the nickel/CB3 under contract. Alex Austin not tendered but was only CB5. CB is not a need — no adjustment required.
Jesse Luketa signed (1yr minimum) adds depth. Jahlani Tavai released but was rotational. Jack Gibbens (RFA, expected to be retained) is the key LB depth piece. No starter-level hole — LB is fine.

key_additions.headline: Former 14th-overall pick fills the LG void left by Jared Wilson's move to center — a high-upside, high-risk bet on a guard who has missed 41 of 85 career games
Versatile 281-lb edge rusher with career-high 7 sacks in 2025 immediately becomes a starter and bolsters a pass rush that ranked 26th in the NFL
Arguably the NFL's best fullback in 2025 fills a schematic gap Josh McDaniels identified as a missing ingredient for the offense
QB3 retained behind Drake Maye and Joshua Dobbs, keeping the quarterback room stable heading into 2026
Minimum-deal depth add to the EDGE room, which now has six players under contract including Harold Landry and Dre'Mont Jones

key_losses.contract_summary: Released — $9.7M dead cap (saved $15M vs. $26.5M 2026 cap hit)
key_losses.headline: Patriots' only 1,000-yard receiver since Julian Edelman (2019) is cut for cap relief, leaving Drake Maye without an established WR1 and creating the team's most urgent offseason need
Fan-favorite nose tackle who blossomed with career-high pressures and was a Swiss Army knife (pass rusher, run stuffer, short-yardage FB) departs, leaving a real hole at IDL
Starting center who played all 21 games in 2025 is traded, triggering Jared Wilson's move from LG to C and the Vera-Tucker acquisition
Capable backup LT who filled in admirably for injured Will Campbell leaves New England with thin depth behind two tackle spots that each carry their own health/age concerns
Veteran TE2 returns to Atlanta, leaving Hunter Henry with minimal proven depth behind him heading into the final year of his contract
Patriots' most productive pass rusher in 2025 (7.5 sacks, 74 total pressures, Super Bowl run) hits the open market — his potential departure is the most damaging unresolved departure on the roster
Breakout 2025 starter (4 INTs, 91.3% snap share) remains unsigned; Patriots are in active discussions but a deal is not yet confirmed as of March 10

notes: Jaylinn Hawkins and K'Lavon Chaisson statuses are UNRESOLVED as of 2026-03-10. Patriots are in active talks with Hawkins; Chaisson's market is being explored. Neither has signed elsewhere or re-signed. The needs analysis conservatively holds their positions at escalated tiers pending resolution.

Alijah Vera-Tucker guarantee details not fully disclosed as of publication date. Contract total value of $42M with max $48M per Adam Schefter/ESPN. Guarantees estimated based on structure and AAV — exact figure TBD.
Dre'Mont Jones guarantee of $23.1M confirmed by ESPN's Dan Graziano; all guaranteed money front-loaded in first two years per report.
The Bradbury trade (for 2027 5th-round pick) costs NE a starter but triggers the IOL reshuffling (Wilson to C, Vera-Tucker at LG) that together constitute the IOL strategy.
WR remains the team's #1 unaddressed need entering the draft. Romeo Doubs interest described as 'unlikely' per Pats Pulpit. AJ Brown trade ask (1st + 2nd) considered too high by NE front office.
